headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 release dominated the window with a wave of autonomous-agent demonstrations.
body: Anthropic released the Mythos model family alongside Fable 5, described as Mythos 5 with guardrails and offered at no added cost across Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22 before shifting to usage credits. Demonstrations showed the model playing Factorio autonomously, simulating the solar system to predict eclipses, generating fluid simulations set to music, and building a CAD editor with an embedded copilot. On the infrastructure side, breakouts cluster around distributed training and scaling, with context parallelism, fully sharded data parallelism, and auto-scaling emerging as new entrants, plus a RunPod framework for deploying GPU workloads directly from an IDE. Several privacy and reasoning techniques, including encrypted reasoning, privacy-preserving AI, and prompt chaining, are lagging or weakening.

cited_items.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
